title: $componentproperties
html:
$componentproperties

Return or set the definition-time widget properties of the current component instance.

$componentproperties { ( ) }

$componentproperties { ( ) } = PropertyValuesList

Example: $componentproperties() = "Frame=T;forecolor=blue"

Parameters

It takes no parameter as input and its only applied on the current running instance.

Data Type Description
PropertyValuesList String Associative list of Property=PropertyValue pairs (separated by GOLD ; ), where PropertyValue is the value to be assigned to the property identified by Property.

Return Values

  • Associative list of widget properties for the current component instance.

  • Empty string ("") if no widget properties have been specified in PropertyValuesList.

If an error occurs, $procerror contains a negative value that identifies the exact error.

Table 1. Values of $procerror Commonly Returned Following Component-Level ProcScript Functions
Value Meaning
-1000 Syntax error (Wrong number of arguments)

Use

Allowed in all component types except self-contained services.

Description

The function $componentproperties sets or returns the definition-time widget properties (as part of the webdefinition JSON stream) for the current component instance.

Widget properties can be explicitly set declaratively in the Uniface IDE or dynamically using $componentproperties; only the current instance is affected.

At runtime, all properties associated with a widget can be changed until the component is presented to the user. After this point, only the widget’s dynamic properties can be modified.

$componentproperties returns only properties that have been explicitly set, either by a previous ProcScript function or declaratively in the Uniface IDE.

For each widget property, Uniface first looks for it in the property string returned by $componentproperties. If the property is not found there, it is then sought in the declarative properties, and finally in the .ini properties.

$componentproperties in UX Component Widgets

When working with UX component widgets, a definition-time widget property can be set in several places. These properties influence how widgets are rendered and behave, following a specific inheritance order.

You can define a widget property in the following ways:

  • web.ini file – Defined in web.ini for the component physical widget.
  • usys.ini file – Defined in usys.ini for the logical widget.
  • Base component – Defined in the base component from which the current component inherits (this is also Property Inspector property).
  • Component PI – Defined in the Property Inspector (PI) of the current component.
  • $componentproperties – Defined dynamically via the $componentproperties.

The inheritance order for these definitions is as follows (from lowest to highest precedence):

web.ini > usys.ini > base component PI > current component PI > $componentproperties

$componentproperties in a Web Application

Style references are stored as property values in the Repository, and can be manipulated with $componentproperties and $instanceproperties. For dynamic changes to the look and feel of style attributes, use the subclass component-level property, which is checked at runtime and propagated through the generated HTML. The subclass property uses predefined style references from the Cascading Style Sheet (CSS) used by the Web application.

The syntax used by $componentproperties is: $componentproperties( )="subclass= MyClass", where MyClass is a predefined style class in the application’s CSS.

Example: Using $componentproperties

This example demonstrates how two instances of the same component can have different background colors by setting $componentproperties based on the instance name.

// In DSP "MYDSP"
operation exec 
  newinstance "ComponentName", "compInstance1"
  newinstance "ComponentName", "compInstance2"
  activate "compInstance1".compOper()
  activate "compInstance2".compOper()
end
// In DSP "ComponentName"
operation compOper
  if($instancename == "compInstance1")
    ;for compInstance1 sets backgroundColor=blue
    $componentproperties() = "backgroundColor=blue"
  else if($instancename == "compInstance2")
    ;for compInstance2 sets backgroundColor=green
    $componentproperties() = "backgroundColor=green"
  endif
end
Related Links

    Return or set the current runtime widget properties of current instance.
